title: centaurea margaritacea subsp. appendicata
intro: Centaurea margaritacea subsp. appendicata, also known as mountain cornflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its high levels of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea margaritacea subsp. appendicata include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy. Centaurea margaritacea subsp. appendicata can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
